newsArray.title: Shree Charani Becomes India's Leading Wicket-Taker in a Single ICC Women's T20 World Cup Edition
newsArray.description: Indian spinner Shree Charani, 21, has equalled the national record for most wickets in a single ICC Women's T20 World Cup edition with 10 scalps in just three matches. As India Women face Bangladesh Women today, June 25, 2026, at Old Trafford, Charani is poised to break this record and become the outright leading wicket-taker for India in a single.
creator: sports desk

newsArray.title: Blind Women Cricketers From MP Await Premiere Of Film On Their Journey
newsArray.description: Sushma said she especially wants her father, a farmer, to watch the film. Sunita Sarathe, from Nayagaon in Narmadapuram district, said she had overcome several challenges in life. "I am blind. My father was a farm labourer. People used to pity me," she said. She expressed hope that the film would inspire people facing any kind of challenge to work hard and realise their dreams.
keywords: from mp await premiere of film on their journey
creator: staff reporter

newsArray.description: By Breaking Belize News Staff (HP): There was plenty to celebrate for Belize cricket, as the national side claimed their first victory of the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup Americas Sub-Regional Qualifier, and saw one of their batsmen rewrite the record books in the process.
